sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before/after to see what changed after RIGHT
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

# Find the player (M magenta object) in before and after
def find_player(seg):
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 18:
            return n
    return None

bp = find_player(before)
ap = find_player(after)
print("Before player boundary: %s" % bp['boundary'] if bp else "Not found")
print("After player boundary: %s" % ap['boundary'] if ap else "Not found")

# Check for any other changes
print("Board changed: last_action_result['board_changed'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{
  "world_model": "Level 1. Player is the magenta object. RIGHT moved the player rightward. Need to see exact displacement.",
  "goal_model": "Probably navigate player to colored squares on the right side.",
  "action_model": "UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT move player moves. MOUSE and ACTION7 available.",
  "recent_findings": ["RIGHT action executed, board changed"],
  "open_questions": ["How far did player move?", "What is the goal?"],
  "current_plan": "Continue moving right towards colored squares",
  "cross_level_notes": ""
}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
